description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body and flat, circular ends. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by three prominent dark metal hoops that encircle the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom. The wood surface displays a natural, weathered grain with subtle variations in brown tones, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The metal bands are slightly tarnished, adding to the rustic and utilitarian appearance. The top of the barrel is fully enclosed with a wooden lid, flush with the rim, and there are no visible handles or spouts. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, serving as a container for goods or waste.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops and a flat, closed top, showing rustic textures and no visible handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full circular top and the three metal hoops, with wood grain and no visible openings or handles.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the barrel and the continuous metal bands, with consistent wood texture.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the bulging midsection and the even spacing of the metal hoops, with no additional features.
description_view_3: Another angled view highlighting the seamless construction of the staves and the aged appearance of the metal bands.
